title: Accounts
lede: Accounts are the companies you do business with. Group every contact, lead, call, email, and file that belongs to one organization under a single company profile — so the next time that business calls, your whole team (and your AI agents) see the full picture instantly.

steps.title: Open the Accounts list
steps.content:
If you sell to businesses, Accounts are your home base: one account (the company) holds many contacts (the people who work there) and all the leads, calls, and messages tied to it.

  1. In the header, click the CRM section, then click the Accounts sub-tab (or go directly to /crm/accounts). The counter on the tab shows your total number of accounts.
  2. Each row is one company. The columns are:
    • Logo — the company logo (a colored monogram until you upload one).
    • Name — click it to open the company profile.
    • Contacts / Leads — how many people and prospects are linked to this account.
    • Phone — the account's first phone number; click it to call through the web dialer. A +N more pill lists additional numbers.
    • Stage — the lifecycle stage pill (stages come from your CRM Settings).
    • Last Updated — when the record last changed.
    • Actions — 👁 view the profile, 📞 call the account, 🗑 delete it.
⚠️ Careful with delete: deleting an account also removes the contacts and leads associated with it. iMash asks you to confirm first.

images.alt: Accounts list showing companies with contacts, leads, phone, and stage columns
images.caption: The Accounts list — every company with its linked contacts, leads, phone, and stage

images.alt: Accounts list showing companies with contacts, leads, phone, and stage columns
images.caption: The Accounts list — every company with its linked contacts, leads, phone, and stage

steps.title: Create a new account
steps.content:
1. Click + Add Account at the top-right of the list. The Create Company drawer opens.
  2. Enter the Company Name — this is the only required field.
  3. Fill in the optional details:
    • Website — the company's site URL.
    • Phone Numbers — click Add Phone Number, type the number, and pick a label (Office, Main, Headquarters, Branch, Fax, Support, Sales, or Other). Add as many as you need.
    • Industry — pick from the industry list.
    • Emails — click Add Email; you can store several addresses, and the first one becomes the account's primary email.
    • Street Address, Apartment/Suite, City, State/Province, Postal Code, Country — the company's address. Selecting United States as the country turns State/Province into a state picker.
  4. Click Save. The drawer closes and your new company appears in the list.
💡 Don't worry about getting everything perfect now — every field (and more) can be edited later, right on the account profile.

images.alt: Create Company drawer with name, website, phone numbers, industry, emails, and address fields
images.caption: The Create Company drawer — only the Company Name is required

images.alt: Create Company drawer with name, website, phone numbers, industry, emails, and address fields
images.caption: The Create Company drawer — only the Company Name is required

steps.title: Search and sort the list
steps.content:
1. Use the dropdown to the left of the search box to choose what to search by: Name, Email, Phone, or Address.
  2. Type in the Search Accounts box — results update as you type. Phone search is smart: it matches digits even if the stored number is formatted differently.
  3. Click a column header (Name, Stage, Last Updated, and more) to sort by it; click again to flip between ascending ↑ and descending ↓.
  4. Use the pagination controls under the table to move between pages and change the page size.

images.alt: Accounts toolbar with search field selector, search box, Filter By dropdown, import, and Add Account buttons
images.caption: The toolbar: search-by selector, search box, Filter By, import, Add Account, and API buttons

images.alt: Accounts toolbar with search field selector, search box, Filter By dropdown, import, and Add Account buttons
images.caption: The toolbar: search-by selector, search box, Filter By, import, Add Account, and API buttons

steps.title: Filter by stage, tags, address, city, or owner
steps.content:
1. Click the Filter By dropdown and tick one or more filter types:
    • Stage — adds a Select Stage dropdown listing your lifecycle stages.
    • Tags — adds a searchable Select Tag dropdown with every tag used on your accounts. Great for grouping companies by territory, industry, or priority.
    • Address — adds a free-text box that matches street, city, state, postal code, or country.
    • City — adds a city search box.
    • Owner — filters by which team member owns the record.
  2. Each filter you enable also adds its matching column to the table (Tags, Address, City), so you can see the values at a glance.
  3. Clear a filter by unticking it in Filter By, or clear its value with the ✕ in its control.

images.alt: Toolbar with Stage and Tags filters enabled showing the extra filter dropdowns
images.caption: Enable Stage and Tags in Filter By and their dropdowns appear next to the search box

images.alt: Toolbar with Stage and Tags filters enabled showing the extra filter dropdowns
images.caption: Enable Stage and Tags in Filter By and their dropdowns appear next to the search box

steps.title: Open the account profile
steps.content:
The account profile is mission control for one company — everything your team knows about the business on a single screen.

  1. Click the account's Name (or the 👁 view icon) in the list.
  2. At the top you'll see the company logo and name:
    • Hover over the logo and click the camera icon to upload a logo (you can remove it later).
    • Click the ✏️ pencil next to the name to rename the account, then click Save.
  3. The page is split in two: tabs on the left (Call Logs, Contacts, Leads, Email, Files, Payment Methods) with a Notes card below, and info cards on the right (Custom Fields, Contact Information, Address, Sales Tax, Account Details).

images.alt: Account profile page with tabs on the left and information cards on the right
images.caption: The account profile: activity tabs on the left, company info cards on the right

images.alt: Account profile page with tabs on the left and information cards on the right
images.caption: The account profile: activity tabs on the left, company info cards on the right

steps.title: Link contacts and leads to the account
steps.content:
This is where accounts earn their keep — all the people and prospects of one company, together.

  1. Open the Contacts tab and click Add Contact to link a person to this company. Each row shows their name, phone (click to call), and stage, with a View button that jumps to their contact profile. View All Contacts for this Account opens the filtered Contacts list.
  2. The Leads tab works exactly the same — click Add Lead to attach prospects, and open any lead with View.
  3. You can also link from the other side: when creating or editing a Contact or Lead, pick the company in its Account field — or select several records in the Leads/Contacts list and assign them to an account in bulk.
💡 The Contacts and Leads counters on the Accounts list update automatically as you link people — a quick health check of how well-mapped each company is.

images.alt: Contacts tab of an account showing a linked contact with phone, stage, and View button
images.caption: The Contacts tab: everyone who works at this company, one click from their profile

images.alt: Contacts tab of an account showing a linked contact with phone, stage, and View button
images.caption: The Contacts tab: everyone who works at this company, one click from their profile

steps.title: Review calls, emails, files, and payment methods
steps.content:
The remaining tabs collect every interaction with the company automatically:

  • Call Logs — every AI and human call with anyone at this account: date, caller, number, stage transition, duration, and status. Click the refresh icon to pull the latest calls.
  • Email — the email conversation history with this account. A blue badge shows the total message count and a green badge highlights unread messages.
  • Files — documents attached to the account (contracts, proposals, photos). Upload here and they stay with the company record.
  • Payment Methods — cards saved for this account, used for invoices and subscriptions.

images.alt: Account profile tab strip showing Call Logs, Contacts, Leads, Email, Files, and Payment Methods
images.caption: One tab strip, the whole relationship: calls, people, emails, files, and payment methods

images.alt: Account profile tab strip showing Call Logs, Contacts, Leads, Email, Files, and Payment Methods
images.caption: One tab strip, the whole relationship: calls, people, emails, files, and payment methods

steps.title: Edit contact information and address
steps.content:
The right-hand cards are edit-in-place — click the ✏️ pencil on any row, change the value, and confirm with the ✓ button.

  1. In the Contact Information card:
    • Phone Numbers — pick a label, type the number, and click Add. Each saved number shows as a pill with its label; click the 🗑 icon to remove one.
    • Emails — click Add Email to store addresses; the first is the primary.
    • Industry and Website — click the pencil to edit.
  2. In the Address card, edit Street Address, Apartment Suite, City, State Province, Postal Code, and Country the same way.

images.alt: Contact Information card with phone numbers, emails, industry, and website
images.caption: Contact Information: phones with labels, emails, industry, and website

images.alt: Contact Information card with phone numbers, emails, industry, and website
images.caption: Contact Information: phones with labels, emails, industry, and website

images.alt: Address card with street, city, state, postal code, and country fields
images.caption: The Address card — every field edits in place

images.alt: Address card with street, city, state, postal code, and country fields
images.caption: The Address card — every field edits in place

steps.title: Fill in company details and sales tax
steps.content:
1. The Account Details card holds the business profile — click the pencil on any row to set it:
    • Company Size — employee-count range.
    • Total Revenue — annual revenue.
    • Primary Contact — your main point of contact.
    • Year Founded, Tax Registration, Lead Source, Tags, Social Media.
  2. In the Sales Tax card, switch on Tax-exempt customer if this company shouldn't be charged sales tax — quotes and invoices for this account will respect it automatically.
💡 Add Tags here (for example VIP, West Coast, Reseller) and they become filterable from the Accounts list via Filter By → Tags.

images.alt: Account Details card with company size, total revenue, primary contact, year founded, tax registration, lead source, tags, and social media
images.caption: Account Details: the full business profile, editable in place

images.alt: Account Details card with company size, total revenue, primary contact, year founded, tax registration, lead source, tags, and social media
images.caption: Account Details: the full business profile, editable in place

images.alt: Sales Tax card with the tax-exempt customer toggle
images.caption: Flip Tax-exempt customer and billing documents for this account skip sales tax

images.alt: Sales Tax card with the tax-exempt customer toggle
images.caption: Flip Tax-exempt customer and billing documents for this account skip sales tax

steps.title: Add notes and custom fields
steps.content:
1. Below the tabs, type in the Add your note box and press Enter to log a note on the account. Notes keep a running team journal — every entry shows who wrote it and when, and can be edited or deleted later.
  2. In the Custom Fields card (top-right), click Add Field, give it a Field Name and Field Value, and click Add. Use custom fields for anything iMash doesn't have a built-in field for — contract renewal dates, account tiers, internal IDs.
  3. Edit or remove a custom field with the ✏️ and 🗑 icons on its row.

images.alt: Notes card with the add-your-note input
images.caption: The Notes card — a running journal on the company

images.alt: Notes card with the add-your-note input
images.caption: The Notes card — a running journal on the company

images.alt: Custom Fields card with Add Field button and existing custom fields
images.caption: Custom Fields: store anything that matters to your business

images.alt: Custom Fields card with Add Field button and existing custom fields
images.caption: Custom Fields: store anything that matters to your business

steps.title: Import accounts in bulk, clean up, and use the API
steps.content:
1. To bring in a whole book of business at once, click the ⬆ import button (next to + Add Account). The Import Accounts from CSV modal opens — drag in a CSV with headers in the first row. Required fields: Name plus either Email or Phone. Click Next to map your columns and finish the import.
  2. To delete several accounts, tick their checkboxes in the list and click Delete Selected. Remember: this also removes their linked contacts and leads.
  3. Developers: click the 🔗 API Info button to see ready-made API examples for reading and writing accounts programmatically.

That's it — your companies now live in one organized, call-ready CRM. Every new call, message, and deal will attach itself to the right account from here on.

images.alt: Import Accounts from CSV modal with drag-and-drop upload area
images.caption: Import Accounts from CSV — Name plus Email or Phone is all a row needs

images.alt: Import Accounts from CSV modal with drag-and-drop upload area
images.caption: Import Accounts from CSV — Name plus Email or Phone is all a row needs

support.title: Need Help?
support.content:
If you run into any issues or have questions:

  • 📧 Email us at support@imash.io
  • 💬 Join our Discord community
  • 📚 Check out our API Reference
  • 🎥 Watch our video tutorials

metaDescription: Create company accounts in the iMash CRM, link contacts and leads, track calls and emails per company, edit company details in place, and import accounts from CSV.
